# datahub_structured_property (Resource)

**DataHub ✅ | DataHub Cloud ✅**

Creates and manages a DataHub structured property definition.

A structured property defines a typed, named custom property that can be applied to data assets in DataHub (e.g. a `number`-valued `retentionDays` applicable to datasets). This resource manages the *definition* only -- the schema object that declares the property type, cardinality, entity applicability, and allowed values. Applying property values to individual assets is per-asset enrichment and is outside the scope of this provider.

## Naming

`property_id` becomes both the URN suffix (`urn:li:structuredProperty:<property_id>`) and the `qualifiedName`. Use a dotted reverse-DNS form (e.g. `io.acme.privacy.classification`) to avoid collisions and to match the convention used by the DataHub Python SDK. A Terraform-managed property and a Python-SDK-created property with the same `property_id` reference the same DataHub entity.

## Update semantics

DataHub's `updateStructuredProperty` mutation is append-only for `entity_types`, `allowed_values`, and `allowed_entity_types`, and cardinality can only widen from `SINGLE` to `MULTIPLE`. Additive changes (adding elements to these lists, or widening cardinality) are applied in-place. If you remove an element from any of these lists or narrow cardinality from `MULTIPLE` to `SINGLE`, Terraform will destroy and recreate the property. **Destroying a property hard-deletes it and removes all applied values from every asset.**

## Value types

Specify `value_type` as a short name: `string`, `number`, `date`, `urn`, or `rich_text`. When `value_type = "urn"`, use `allowed_entity_types` to restrict which entity types the URN values may reference.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `entity_types` (Set of String) Set of entity type short names this property can be applied to (e.g. `["dataset", "dashboard"]`). Corresponds to `urn:li:entityType:datahub.<name>` URNs. Adding entity types is applied in-place; removing any forces a new resource.
- `property_id` (String) Unique identifier for the structured property. Becomes the URN suffix and `qualifiedName` (`urn:li:structuredProperty:<property_id>`). Use a dotted reverse-DNS form (e.g. `io.acme.privacy.classification`). Changing this forces a new resource.
- `value_type` (String) Data type for values of this property. One of: `string`, `number`, `date`, `urn`, `rich_text`. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `allowed_entity_types` (Set of String) Entity type short names that URN values may reference. Only meaningful when `value_type = "urn"`. E.g. `["corpuser", "corpGroup"]`. Adding entity types is applied in-place; removing any forces a new resource.
- `allowed_values` (Attributes List) Optional list of allowed values. When set, only values in this list may be applied to assets. Each entry specifies exactly one of `string_value` or `number_value` and an optional `description`. Adding values is applied in-place; removing any forces a new resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--allowed_values))
- `cardinality` (String) Whether each asset may have one (`SINGLE`) or multiple (`MULTIPLE`) values for this property. Defaults to `SINGLE`. Widening from `SINGLE` to `MULTIPLE` is applied in-place; narrowing forces a new resource.
- `description` (String) Description of the property's purpose and intended usage.
- `display_name` (String) Human-readable display name shown in the DataHub UI. Defaults to `property_id` if not set.
- `immutable` (Boolean) Whether values applied to assets are immutable once set. Defaults to `false`.
- `settings` (Attributes) Display and search settings for this property in the DataHub UI. If `is_hidden` is true, all other flags must be false. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--settings))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `qualified_name` (String) The qualified name of the property (equal to `property_id`).
- `urn` (String) Full DataHub URN for this structured property (e.g., `urn:li:structuredProperty:io.acme.retention`).

<a id="nestedatt--allowed_values"></a>
### Nested Schema for `allowed_values`

Optional:

- `description` (String) Human-readable description of what this value means.
- `number_value` (Number) Numeric allowed value. Set exactly one of `string_value` or `number_value`.
- `string_value` (String) String allowed value. Set exactly one of `string_value` or `number_value`.


<a id="nestedatt--settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `settings`

Optional:

- `hide_in_asset_summary_when_empty` (Boolean) Hide the property from the asset summary when no value is set. Defaults to `false`.
- `is_hidden` (Boolean) Hide this property from the DataHub UI entirely. Defaults to `false`.
- `show_as_asset_badge` (Boolean) Show the property value as a badge on asset cards. Defaults to `false`.
- `show_in_asset_summary` (Boolean) Show this property in the asset summary panel. Defaults to `false`.
- `show_in_columns_table` (Boolean) Show the property in the schema columns table. Defaults to `false`.
- `show_in_search_filters` (Boolean) Show this property as a search filter facet. Defaults to `false`.
